judgeScores.justification: The parser demonstrates clean code organization and thoughtful features like automatic type coercion and convenient aggregation methods (sum, average, groupBy). However, it critically fails to implement two explicit requirements: handling newlines within quoted fields (it pre-splits input on line separators before parsing, breaking multiline fields) and streaming support for large files. Additionally, it lacks the requested TypeScript generics for type-safe row parsing, instead relying on `Record<string, any>`.
